headline: Super vs Gemini: personal AI agents for real computer work

site_brief.goal: Create a fair but persuasive competitor comparison page for Super vs Gemini. Use supplied sources for current facts, avoid fake claims, and make the practical argument that Super is better suited for repeated computer-use work because its cache can lower repeated execution cost. Include a long-form buyer guide with a decision matrix, migration checklist, how-to evaluation plan, and source-backed caveats. Previous comparison-page failures omitted visible buyer-guide and decision-matrix language even when the article was useful; that must never happen. The visible page must include the exact phrases Buyer guide and Decision matrix.
site_brief.title: Super vs Gemini

summary: A fair, source‑backed comparison for people choosing between Super and Google Gemini. Gemini is pushing computer‑use agents on macOS with Spark and new computer‑use models. Super focuses on durable personal AI agents that actually operate computers and reuse a computer‑use cache so repeated workflows get faster and cheaper over time. This page explains when Gemini fits, when Super is sharper, and how to evaluate both for repeated computer‑use workflows.
